mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals deteriorating consumer spending power and corporate earnings pressure, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and generates positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.05 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, which compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the mechanism directional accuracy drops below 45% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real purchasing power and force central banks to tighten policy, increasing discount rates and producing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show no statistically significant negative correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates healthy labor market absorption without wage-price spirals, supporting sustained corporate earnings and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion signals fading economic momentum, prompting equity market repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration fails to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates acute supply-side weakness, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases when INDPRO falls below its 12-month average, or the threshold signal shows no predictive edge in out-of-sample testing.
